title: OpenAI Silent Failure Diagnosis & Fix
summary: Fix silent failures when OpenAI models (gpt-5.3-codex, gpt-5.4-mini) respond through oc-chatgpt-multi-auth plugin. Three root cause candidates: SSE fallback returning raw text, 45s stream stall timeout, and gpt-5.4-mini model name being stripped.
keyOutcomes: Confirmed the SSE fallback issue: missing terminal response events previously returned raw SSE text with HTTP 200.
Patched response-handler.js so missing response.done returns parseable JSON error with HTTP 502.
Increased default stream stall timeout to 120000 ms while preserving CODEX_AUTH_STREAM_STALL_TIMEOUT_MS override.
Added explicit gpt-5.4-mini model-map entries so mini is preserved instead of normalized to gpt-5.4.
Added DEBUG_CHATGPT_PROXY diagnostics for request summaries and SSE conversion events.
Module-level validation passed for syntax, timeout default, model preservation, JSON error response, and debug logging.
Follow-up live validation found a broader missing arguments case: tool_search_call items in native transform mode also require arguments.
Patched fetch-helpers.js with a last-mile call-like input sanitizer and removed an ESM-unsafe debug_body writer.
risks: Plugin modifications may be overwritten on npm update (same risk as previous arguments fix)
Live OpenCode Desktop validation is still required to prove no silent fallback occurs in the real authenticated model path
deliverables: Diagnostic logging in request-transformer.js and response-handler.js
validationCriteria: At least one OpenAI model completes a full agent loop without fallback
Errors are visible in OpenCode logs when failures occur
gpt-5.4-mini model name preserved in requests
